title:
WHAT DID YOU DO?
& PAST SIMPLE
subtitle: Grammar & Listening · A2 · ~45 min

blocks.title: 📘 PAST SIMPLE · acciones terminadas
blocks.intro: Usamos el past simple para acciones terminadas en el pasado (ayer, la semana pasada, en 2021). Repasa y luego practica con varios ejercicios.

cells.body:
didn't + verbo base
I didn't work
she didn't go (no "didn't went")

Did you work?
What did you do?
Yes, I did. / No, I didn't.

She went home.
She goed home.
They were late. (was/were = pasado de "to be")

blocks.note: Cómo funciona: esta lección mezcla completar frases 📝, escucha 🎧 y ordenar palabras 🧩. Haz el ✓ CHECK en cada ejercicio.

blocks.title: ★ STORIES FROM THE TEAM
blocks.intro: Marcos habla del pasado: su trabajo anterior y el primer gran proyecto del equipo. Primero practica las formas, después escucha las historias.

blocks.title: COMPLETA EN PASADO
blocks.intro: Elige la forma correcta del pasado simple.

items.q: Yesterday I ___ to the office early.
He ___ go to work yesterday — it was a holiday.
We ___ a problem, but we fixed it.

blocks.title: AUDIO 1 · MARCOS'S LAST JOB
blocks.desc: Sofia entrevista a Marcos sobre su trabajo anterior. Fíjate en los verbos en pasado.

options: At a small startup in Mexico City
At a big bank in London

questions.q: What did he do at his last job?
options: He built websites and fixed bugs

questions.q: Marcos studied at university for four years.

speaking.prompt: Describe Marcos's last job. Use worked, built and studied.
speaking.starter: He worked at a small startup for two years, where he built…

transcript.html: So Marcos, tell me about your last job. Where did you work before?
I worked at a small startup in Mexico City for two years. I started there in 2021.
Nice! What did you do there?
I built websites and fixed bugs. I also helped the new developers.
The big project finished and the team got smaller. So I looked for a new job and found this one.
Yes, I studied at university for four years, and I graduated in 2019.

blocks.title: ORDENA LA FRASE EN PASADO
blocks.intro: Toca las palabras para formar la frase correcta.

blocks.title: AUDIO 2 · THE BIG LAUNCH
blocks.desc: Sofia cuenta el primer gran lanzamiento del equipo. Escucha el orden: first, then, after that.

questions.q: How long did the team plan the app?
What did they do the night before the launch?
options: They worked late and ordered pizza

questions.q: Thousands of people downloaded the app on the first day.

speaking.prompt: Tell the launch story in order. Use first, then and after that.
speaking.starter: First, the team planned the app for three months. Then they…

transcript.html: Two years ago, we created a new app. First, we planned everything for three months. Then we wrote the code. After that, we tested it. The night before, we worked late and ordered pizza. The next morning, we launched the app. Thousands of people downloaded it on the first day! The client loved it, and we celebrated.

blocks.title: ✍️ MY LAST JOB / A PAST PROJECT
blocks.intro: Cuenta (5–6 frases) sobre tu último trabajo o un proyecto, en past simple:
checklist: Di dónde trabajaste y qué hacías (I worked…, I made…)
Usa al menos 2 verbos irregulares (went, had, made, got, found…)
Usa un marcador de tiempo (last year, two years ago, in 2021)
blocks.placeholder: Two years ago, I worked at...
blocks.example: (Time) ago, I worked at (place). I started (when) and (past action). I had (something / someone). One day, (a problem), but (how you fixed it). I left the job (when) to (reason).
blocks.hw: Escribe tu historia y graba un audio leyéndola en voz alta. Envíame ambos por WhatsApp y lo corregimos.

lines: So Marcos, tell me about your last job. Where did you work before?
I worked at a small startup in Mexico City for two years. I started there in 2021.
Nice! What did you do there?
I built websites and fixed bugs. I also helped the new developers on the team.
The big project finished and the team got smaller. So I looked for a new job, and I found this one.
Yes, I studied at university for four years, and I graduated in 2019.

Two years ago, we created a new app for a client. First, we planned everything for three months. Then we designed the app and wrote the code. After that, we tested it many times. The night before the launch, we worked late and ordered pizza. The next morning, we launched the app. Thousands of people downloaded it on the very first day! The client loved it, and the whole team celebrated.
